title: Lines in Three Dimensions
summary: Equations of lines in 3D space (vector and parametric form), distance between lines, and intersections.
content_html:
Equation of a Line in 3D

A line through point P₀ = (x₀, y₀, z₀) with direction vector d = (d₁, d₂, d₃):

Vector form:

r(t) = P₀ + t·d = (x₀ + td₁, y₀ + td₂, z₀ + td₃),  t ∈ ℝ

Parametric equations:

x = x₀ + td₁
y = y₀ + td₂
z = z₀ + td₃

Symmetric (Cartesian) form (when d₁,d₂,d₃ ≠ 0):

(x-x₀)/d₁ = (y-y₀)/d₂ = (z-z₀)/d₃ = t

Example

Line through (1,2,-1) with direction d=(2,1,3):
r(t) = (1+2t, 2+t, -1+3t)
Symmetric: (x-1)/2 = (y-2)/1 = (z+1)/3

Angle Between Two Lines

Lines with directions d₁ and d₂ make angle θ where:

cos(θ) = |d₁·d₂| / (|d₁||d₂|)

(Use absolute value since we want the acute angle.)

Intersection of Two Lines

Lines L₁: r = a + sd, L₂: r = b + te. Check if there exist s,t such that a + sd = b + te (solve the system). Three cases:

  • Unique intersection: consistent with unique solution for s,t.
  • Parallel lines: directions are proportional, lines don't meet.
  • Skew lines: not parallel, but no intersection (in 3D only).

Distance from a Point to a Line

Point Q, line through A with direction d:

distance = |(Q-A) × d| / |d|

Distance Between Two Skew Lines

Lines with directions d₁, d₂ and points A₁, A₂:

n = d₁ × d₂  (perpendicular to both)
distance = |(A₂-A₁) · n| / |n|

exercises.prompt: A line through (1,0,2) with direction (3,1,-1) in parametric form is:

exercises.title: Skew lines
options: Not parallel and do not intersect
exercises.answer: Not parallel and do not intersect

exercises.title: Parameter t in line equation
exercises.prompt: In r(t) = P₀ + td, the parameter t represents:
options: How far along the line we are from P₀
exercises.answer: How far along the line we are from P₀
